mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes versus 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns do not significantly exceed negative returns during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which boosts corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to generate positive forward returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism reverses direction during high-inflation regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly supports consumer discretionary spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS changes versus 20-day returns remains below 0.01, or positive payroll surprises fail to predict positive market returns with greater than 55% accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% signals accelerating inflation that forces monetary tightening expectations, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations to produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of high-inflation threshold crossings, or the negative predictive relationship breaks down during supply-driven inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms synchronized macroeconomic expansion, which reduces recession risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to outperform single-series predictors, or forward returns turn negative in more than 50% of synchronized growth quarters despite the positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of rising unemployment indicates a broad economic contraction, which triggers earnings downgrades and defensive portfolio shifts that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint negative signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the mechanism loses predictive significance when tested against a baseline model excluding the interaction term.
